complexity_weights._note: Complexity score is a weighted sum of min-max normalized cost components: trainable parameters, wall-clock time in hours, and input-size ratio. Lower is better; clipping keeps the diagnostic score finite and comparable for publication tables.

test_selection_policy.notes: Per-tag candidate preselection and final backbone/run selection are both validation-only and both use the same true three-objective Pareto rule over validation error, runtime, and trainable parameters. After selection, the final model is retrained on train+val with fixed epochs and no validation-driven callbacks before a single held-out test evaluation. Ablations remain validation-only and are excluded from final test evaluation.
test_selection_policy.baseline_note: Literature-style baselines include a frozen feature extractor, a standard partial fine-tuning recipe, and a small manual tuning grid without GA/Bayesian search.
test_selection_policy.selection_note: Search optimizes mean validation AUC, mean runtime per run, and mean trainable parameters as three Pareto objectives. Per-tag top-k genome preselection is restricted to Pareto-ranked validation summaries and uses minimum normalized Euclidean distance to the ideal point over validation error, runtime, and trainable parameters within each non-dominated layer. Final backbone/run selection uses the same rule on validation candidates. After selection, the chosen model is retrained on the union of train and validation splits with a fixed two-stage schedule and without validation-based early stopping or learning-rate adaptation.
